content: Hey, I'm thinking of learning some new skills outside of being a Pastry Chef. I've been watching a lot of YouTube videos on beekeeping and I'm really interested in trying it out.
That's fascinating! Beekeeping can be a very rewarding hobby. What is it about beekeeping that interests you the most?

I think it's the combination of science and nature. I've always been interested in how things work and beekeeping seems like a great way to learn about that. Plus, I love the idea of having my own honey to use in my recipes at the Michelin-starred restaurant where I work.
Having your own honey to use in your recipes would definitely be a unique touch. Have you considered taking a beekeeping course or joining a local beekeeping community to learn more?

Yeah, I've been looking into that. There's a great course at the local community center that starts on March 15th and I'm thinking of signing up. I've also been talking to my friend, Alex, who's an experienced beekeeper and he's offered to mentor me.

That sounds like a great plan! Having a mentor can be really helpful when learning a new skill. What do you think you'll use your new beekeeping skills for, aside from using the honey in your recipes?

I'm not sure yet, but I'm thinking maybe I'll start selling my own honey at the local farmer's market. I've also been considering starting a YouTube channel to document my beekeeping journey and share my recipes with others.
Starting a YouTube channel can be a great way to share your passion with others. What kind of content do you think you'll feature on your channel, aside from beekeeping and recipes?
I'm thinking maybe some videos on triathlon training, since I'm also really into that. And maybe some tutorials on pottery, since I've recently taken up that hobby as well.

That sounds like a great idea! It's always interesting to see how people balance multiple hobbies and interests. Do you find that your experience as a Pastry Chef helps you with your pottery or triathlon training in any way?

Actually, yeah! My attention to detail as a Pastry Chef has really helped me with my pottery. And my experience with triathlon training has helped me with time management and discipline, which has been really helpful in my pastry career.

That's really interesting. It's great to see how different skills and hobbies can complement each other. What do you think is the most challenging part of learning a new skill, like beekeeping or pottery?

For me, it's probably just finding the time to dedicate to it. As a Pastry Chef, my schedule can be pretty demanding, especially during peak season. But I'm hoping that having a YouTube channel will help me stay motivated and accountable.
Having a public platform can definitely be a great motivator. Do you have any plans for your first YouTube video, or is that still in the works?

I'm thinking of doing a video on my favorite recipe for honey cake, and then maybe a follow-up video on my beekeeping journey so far. I'm excited to share my passions with others and see where this new project takes me.
